overview: The YAML Filter processes [YAML files](http://www.yaml.org/) and supports [Ruby on Rails](http://rubyonrails.org/) message variables. It extracts translatable string values from YAML key-value pairs, lists, and maps, assigning each entry a name composed of its full parent key path (e.g., `fr/activerecord/errors/messages/exclusion`). The filter handles Unicode BOM detection for encoding and preserves original line-break types in output.

extraction.extractAll.help:
Controls the default extraction behavior for key/string pairs.

- **When enabled**: all key/string pairs are extracted, *except* those matching the `excludeKeys` pattern.
- **When disabled**: no key/string pairs are extracted, *except* those matching the `excludeKeys` pattern (inverted logic).

This works as a toggle that flips the meaning of the `excludeKeys` regex — matching keys become exceptions to whichever default you choose.

extraction.includeIsolatedStrings.help:
Extracts strings that are **not associated with a key**, such as bare string values in YAML arrays or lists.

For example, in:
```yaml
list: [one, two, three]
```
the values `one`, `two`, `three` are isolated strings — they have no direct key association.

extraction.excludeKeys.help:
A regular expression matched against YAML keys. Matching keys behave **opposite** to the default extraction mode:

- If `extractAll` is **enabled**: matching keys are **excluded** from extraction.
- If `extractAll` is **disabled**: matching keys are **included** for extraction.

When `useFullPath` is enabled, the regex is matched against the full key path (e.g., `menu/value/popup/menuitem/value`).
extraction.excludeKeys.values: Java regular expression pattern matched against YAML key names (or full key paths if `useFullPath` is enabled).
examples: `^en/.*` — match all keys under the `en` locale when using full paths

dependsOn.condition: inverts meaning: when extractAll is true, matching keys are excluded; when false, matching keys are included
when enabled, regex matches against full key path instead of immediate key

keys.useAsName.help: Uses the YAML key as the `resname` (resource name) of each extracted text unit. This maps to the `name` attribute in XLIFF output, making entries identifiable by their YAML key rather than an auto-generated ID.

keys.useFullPath.help:
Uses the complete nested key path as the resource name instead of just the immediate key.

For example, for a value nested under `menu > value > popup > menuitem > value`, the name becomes `menu/value/popup/menuitem/value` instead of just `value`.

Also affects how `excludeKeys` regex matching works — when enabled, the regex is matched against the full path.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for useFullPath to take effect

output.escapeNonAscii.help: Escapes non-ASCII characters in the output using Unicode escape sequences. Useful when the target system requires ASCII-safe YAML files.

output.wrap.help: Controls whether long lines are wrapped in the output YAML file.
notes: **Warning:** Wrapped lines in the original source document will be **unwrapped** in the target document regardless of this setting — all text will appear on a single line in the output.

output.subFilterProcessLiteralAsBlock.help:
When sub-filtering is active, controls whether YAML literal block scalars (using `|` syntax) are processed through the sub-filter.

This is useful when literal blocks contain structured content (e.g., HTML) that should be parsed by the sub-filter.

inlineCodes.enabled.help:
Activates pattern-based detection of inline codes within extracted text. Matched patterns are converted to inline code placeholders (like `<ph>` in XLIFF), protecting them from translation.

The default patterns detect:
- **printf-style format specifiers**: `%d`, `%s`, `%2$f`, etc.
- **Escape sequences**: `\n`, `\r`, `\t`, `\a`, `\b`, `\f`, `\v`, `\r\n`
- **Numbered placeholders**: `{0}`, `{1}`, etc.
notes: **Note:** This option cannot be used together with the sub-filtering option. Use one or the other.

inlineCodes.rules.help:
Regular expression patterns that identify inline codes within extracted text. Each match is converted to a placeholder code.

The default expression covers:
```
((%(([-0+#]?)[-0+#]?)(([0-9]\$)?)(([-0-9*]*)(\.[-0-9*]*)?)[dioxXucsfeEgGpn])
|((\\r\\n)|\\a|\\b|\\f|\\n|\\r|\\t|\\v)
|(\{[0-9].*?\}))
```

inlineCodes.mergeAdjacent.help: When multiple inline codes appear next to each other with no translatable text between them, merges them into a single placeholder. This simplifies the segment for translators.

inlineCodes.moveBoundaryCodes.help: Moves inline codes that appear at the very beginning or end of a segment out of the translatable content and into the skeleton. This produces cleaner segments for translation.

inlineCodes.simplifierRules.help: Rules for simplifying the representation of inline codes, reducing complexity for translators by combining or collapsing code patterns.

limitations: Wrapped lines in the original source document will be **unwrapped** in the target document — all text will appear on a single line.

If an illegal character is introduced during translation, the user must manually add double or single quotes to make the target YAML file valid. A WARNING is issued by the filter when this scenario is detected.
Inline code detection (`inlineCodes.enabled`) cannot be used together with sub-filtering — they are mutually exclusive.
processingNotes: **Encoding detection**: If a Unicode BOM is present, the corresponding encoding (UTF-8, UTF-16, etc.) is used. Otherwise, the default encoding specified when opening the document is applied.
**Line breaks**: The output preserves the same line-break type (CRLF, LF) as the original input.
**Entry naming**: Each extracted entry is named using the full path of its parent keys joined by `/` (e.g., `fr/activerecord/errors/messages/exclusion`).

examples.title: Ruby on Rails i18n YAML
examples.description: A typical Rails locale file. All highlighted string values are extracted with full key paths as names.
examples.input:
```yaml
fr:
  activerecord:
    errors:
      template:
        header:
          list: [one, two, three]
          map: {key: value, key2: value2}
          one: "Impossible d'enregistrer {{model}}: 1 erreur"
          other: "Impossible d'enregistrer {{model}}: {{count}} erreurs."
        body: "Veuillez vérifier les champs suivants :"
      messages:
        inclusion: "n'est pas inclus(e) dans la liste"
        exclusion: "n'est pas disponible"
```

examples.title: Inline codes for Ruby interpolation
examples.description: Configure inline code detection to protect Ruby i18n `%{variable}` placeholders from translation.
examples.config:
```yaml
inlineCodes:
  enabled: true
  rules:
    rules:
      - pattern: "%\\{[^}]+\\}"
    sample: "Hello %{name}, you have %{count} messages"
```

extraction.excludeKeys.title: Key Exception Pattern
extraction.excludeKeys.description: Regular expression for keys that behave opposite to the default extraction mode.

extraction.includeIsolatedStrings.title: Extract Isolated Strings
extraction.includeIsolatedStrings.description: Extract string values that are not directly associated with a key.

output.subFilterProcessLiteralAsBlock.title: Sub-filter Literal Blocks
output.subFilterProcessLiteralAsBlock.description: Process YAML literal block scalars through the configured sub-filter.

fullDoc: # YAML Filter The YAML Filter processes [YAML files](http://www.yaml.org/) and supports [Ruby on Rails](http://rubyonrails.org/) message variables. It extracts translatable string values from YAML key-value pairs, lists, and maps, assigning each entry a name composed of its full parent key path (e.g., `fr/activerecord/errors/messages/exclusion`). The filter handles Unicode BOM detection for encoding and preserves original line-break types in output. ## Parameters ### Extraction #### Extract All Key/String Pairs Controls the default extraction behavior for key/string pairs. - **When enabled**: all key/string pairs are extracted, *except* those matching the key exception pattern. - **When disabled**: no key/string pairs are extracted, *except* those matching the key exception pattern (inverted logic). This works as a toggle that flips the meaning of the exception regex. #### Extract Isolated Strings Extracts strings that are **not associated with a key**, such as bare string values in YAML arrays or lists. For example, in: ```yaml list: [one, two, three] ``` the values `one`, `two`, `three` are isolated strings. #### Key Exception Pattern A regular expression matched against YAML keys. Matching keys behave **opposite** to the default extraction mode: - If extractAll is **enabled**: matching keys are **excluded** from extraction. - If extractAll is **disabled**: matching keys are **included** for extraction. When full key path is enabled, the regex is matched against the full path (e.g., `menu/value/popup/menuitem/value`). ### Keys #### Use Key as Name Uses the YAML key as the `resname` (resource name) of each extracted text unit. This maps to the `name` attribute in XLIFF output. #### Use Full Key Path Uses the complete nested key path as the resource name instead of just the immediate key. For example: `fr/activerecord/errors/messages/exclusion` instead of just `exclusion`. > **Note:** Requires "Use Key as Name" to be enabled. ### Output #### Escape Non-ASCII Escapes non-ASCII characters in the output using Unicode escape sequences. #### Line Wrapping Controls whether long lines are wrapped in the output YAML file. > **Warning:** Wrapped lines in the original source will be unwrapped in the target document regardless of this setting. #### Sub-filter Literal Blocks When sub-filtering is active, controls whether YAML literal block scalars (using `|` syntax) are processed through the sub-filter. ### Inline Codes #### Enable Inline Code Detection Activates pattern-based detection of inline codes within extracted text. Matched patterns are converted to inline code placeholders. > **Note:** Cannot be used together with sub-filtering. #### Code Finder Rules Regular expression patterns that identify inline codes. Default patterns detect printf-style specifiers, escape sequences, and numbered placeholders. Presets available: - **Ruby i18n**: `%\{[^}]+\}` — matches `%{name}`, `%{count}` - **HTML**: `<[^>]+>` — matches ``, `` #### Merge Adjacent Codes Merges consecutive inline codes with no translatable text between them into a single placeholder. #### Move Boundary Codes Moves inline codes at the start or end of a segment into the skeleton (non-translatable content). #### Simplifier Rules Rules for simplifying inline code representation for translators. ## Limitations - Wrapped lines in the original source document will be **unwrapped** in the target document — all text appears on a single line. - If an illegal character is introduced during translation, the user must manually add quotes to keep the YAML valid. A WARNING is issued when detected. - Inline code detection cannot be used together with sub-filtering — they are mutually exclusive. ## Notes - **Encoding**: Unicode BOM is detected automatically. Otherwise the default encoding specified when opening the document is used. - **Line breaks**: Output preserves the original line-break type (CRLF, LF). - **Entry naming**: Each entry is named by joining all parent keys with `/` (e.g., `fr/activerecord/errors/messages/exclusion`). ## Examples ### Ruby on Rails i18n file ```yaml fr: activerecord: errors: messages: inclusion: "n'est pas inclus(e) dans la liste" exclusion: "n'est pas disponible" invalid: "n'est pas valide" ``` All string values are extracted with full key paths as names (e.g., `fr/activerecord/errors/messages/exclusion`). ### Inline code detection for Ruby interpolation ```yaml inlineCodes: enabled: true rules: rules: - pattern: "%\\{[^}]+\\}" sample: "Hello %{name}, you have %{count} messages" ``` This protects `%{name}` and `%{count}` placeholders from translation.
